title: Scope Creep / Say-No
description: Small-business boundary rubric when the ask grows: free tweak, fee, defer, re-quote, or no. Use after mf-tpl-004 intake or mid-job.

request.description: What they asked for on top of agreed scope

decision_tree.description: Map for humans. Engine uses decision_rules by priority.

yes_courtesy_once.label: Yes once (courtesy)
yes_courtesy_once.title: Tiny fix, absorb once, document
yes_courtesy_once.summary: Goodwill tweak that does not change deliverable or deadline. Log it so second tweak becomes fee.
next_steps: Do the tiny fix if under 15 minutes.
Reply: happy to help this once; future changes are quoted.
Log in scope log: courtesy_used Y.
yes_courtesy_once.do_not: Make courtesy a pattern. Second medium ask same job = fee.

fee_required.label: Fee required
fee_required.title: Medium change = change order
fee_required.summary: Scope moved. Name price before work starts.
next_steps: Send change-order line: what, hours/cost, new date.
No work until yes in writing (email OK).
Update quote or invoice.

fee_rush_or_decline.label: Rush fee or decline
fee_rush_or_decline.title: Hard deadline plus extra scope
fee_rush_or_decline.summary: Rush surcharge or honest no. Pick one.
next_steps: Quote rush fee + change fee separately.
If cannot hit date, offer new date without rush fee.
Decline if margin thin and risk high.
fee_rush_or_decline.do_not: Heroics that blow the calendar for other clients.

fee_or_no.label: Fee or no
fee_or_no.title: Margin already thin
fee_or_no.summary: You are not obligated to lose money on scope creep.
next_steps: State thin margin honestly (no guilt trip).
Offer paid change order or original scope only.
Walk away polite if they refuse both.
fee_or_no.do_not: Absorb medium/large work to avoid awkward chat.

fee_or_trade.label: Fee or trade
fee_or_trade.title: Friends and family still get clarity
fee_or_trade.summary: Love them; still name cost or trade so resentment does not grow.
next_steps: Same change-order script with warm tone.
Offer trade only if you actually want the trade.
Never trade into resentment.

defer_phase_two.label: Defer to phase 2
defer_phase_two.title: Good idea, wrong phase
defer_phase_two.summary: Park add-on until v1 ships. Keeps focus and cash flow.
next_steps: Acknowledge the idea.
Ship agreed scope first.
Quote phase 2 as separate line item with date.

requote_before_work.label: Re-quote before work
requote_before_work.title: Large change before delivery
requote_before_work.summary: Stop. New spec, new price, new date.
next_steps: Pause production on changed pieces.
Written revised quote.
Customer sign-off on new scope.

requote_new_job.label: New job
requote_new_job.title: Delivered work plus new ask = new engagement
requote_new_job.summary: Past job is closed. This is a fresh intake.
next_steps: Route mf-tpl-004 intake on new ask.
Do not call it a quick favor.
Quote as new job number.
requote_new_job.do_not: Free revision spiral on closed jobs.

freeze_and_document.label: Freeze and document
freeze_and_document.title: No written scope yet
freeze_and_document.summary: Stop verbal drift. Write what is in and out.
next_steps: Email: here is what we agreed so far.
List in-scope and out-of-scope bullets.
No more work until reply confirms.
freeze_and_document.do_not: Keep building while scope is fuzzy.

no_polite.label: No (polite)
no_polite.title: Out of capacity, ethics, or skill
no_polite.summary: Decline without drama. Refer if possible.
next_steps: Short no with reason.
Refer alternate if you have one.
Do not leave thread open ambiguous.

escalate_complaint.label: Escalate to complaint
escalate_complaint.title: Scope talk turned into blame
escalate_complaint.summary: If tone is refund, failure, or threat, switch playbooks.
next_steps: Stop negotiating scope in anger.
mf-tpl-001 recovery path.
Owner on thread.
escalate_complaint.do_not: Argue scope while they are nuclear.

scripts.script-courtesy-once: Happy to adjust [tiny thing] this once. If we need more changes, I will send a quick change-order so timing and cost stay fair for both of us.
scripts.script-fee-change-order: We can add [change]. That is outside the original scope. Change order: [work], [price], [date]. Reply yes and I will proceed.
scripts.script-rush-fee: We can try [deadline] with a rush fee of [amount] plus the change. Without rush fee the realistic date is [date]. Which do you prefer?
scripts.script-thin-margin: I want to be straight: we are tight on margin for this job. I can do [original scope] as quoted, or [change] as a paid add-on of [price]. I cannot absorb [change] for free.
scripts.script-friend-fee: I can do this for you. Even for friends, I need [price or trade] so the shop stays healthy. Cool?
scripts.script-phase-two: Great idea. Let us finish [v1] first (on track for [date]). I will quote [add-on] as phase 2 right after delivery.
scripts.script-requote: This changes the spec enough that I need to re-quote before more work. Pausing on [affected part] until you approve revised scope: [summary].
scripts.script-new-job: Happy to help with [new ask]. That is a new project from the one we delivered [date]. I will send intake questions and a fresh quote.
scripts.script-freeze-scope: Before more work, let me write down what is in scope: [list]. Out of scope unless we add a change order: [list]. Reply confirm or edits.
scripts.script-no-polite: Thank you for asking. This is not a fit for us on [reason]. [Optional referral.]
scripts.script-pause-recovery: I want to fix how this felt for you. I am pausing scope talk so we can address [issue] first. [Owner] will follow up today.

operator_lens.new_client: Set boundary early. Courtesy once max.
operator_lens.repeat: Relationship worth clarity. Log every courtesy.
operator_lens.vip: Extra care, not free medium work.
